use_split.rationale: Use split is directly anchored in the bestemmingsplan's explicit programmatic caps for the Draka terrein: 120,000 m² residential (max_bvo_residential), 12,000 m² productive industry (min_bvo_productive_industry, a hard minimum), 9,000 m² office (max_bvo_office), 3,900 m² social/maatschappelijk (max_bvo_social_functions), 3,500 m² culture (max_bvo_culture_recreation), 3,000 m² horeca (max_bvo_horeca). Total reconciles with max_bvo_total_draka = 151,400 m². Office, horeca, culture, and social are capped; productive is a floor. The combined dienstverlening+horeca+culture ceiling of 6,500 m² (max_bvo_services_combined) is respected (3,000+3,500=6,500). BAG context (1000 buildings within 1km, mixed residential and former industrial) and OSM amenities (schools, shops, supermarkets nearby) confirm a mixed-use programme is contextually appropriate.

reasons: Programme caps explicitly stated in regels and toelichting
All sub-totals reconcile with overall 151,400 m² cap

unit_mix.rationale: Transformation-project social housing share is 30% (programme_social_housing_transformation_30_percent). CBS shows avg household size 1.9 and population skewed to young adults (median age 39.4), supporting smaller social-rent units in the 1br/30-60m² band.

reasons: Typology split is designer inference from CBS household size

unit_mix.rationale: Remaining sociale_huur share allocated to family-suitable 2br units to broaden household mix in a primarily single/2-person buurt.

Middenhuur is 40% of dwellings (programme_middeldure_transformation_40_percent). Minimum GO is 40 m² (min_middeldure_huur_go). Half of middenhuur set as compact 1br to match CBS 1.9 person/household and to address Amsterdam middenhuur demand.
Other half of middenhuur as 2br to provide family-suitable middenhuur stock.

reasons: Tenure share explicit; typology split is judgment

unit_mix.rationale: Vrije sector/dure share is 30% for transformation projects (programme_dure_transformation_30_percent). Majority allocated to mid-size 2br for market demand at this scale.
Remainder of vrije sector as larger 3br family units to diversify household composition.

reasoning_trace.decision: Set target total GFA at 151,400 m² (hard ceiling for the entire Draka programme).
reasoning_trace.evidence: [max_bvo_total_draka] explicitly caps total programme at 151,400 m² bvo; [max_bvo_blokken_a6_a13] confirms the same figure for blokken A6–A13.

reasoning_trace.decision: Allocate 120,000 m² to residential and ≈1,630 dwellings.
reasoning_trace.evidence: [max_bvo_residential] caps residential at 120,000 m²; [target_bvo_woningen] and toelichting state circa 1,630 woningen for 120,000 m² BVO — implies average ≈74 m² GFA per dwelling.

reasoning_trace.decision: Set productive industry at 12,000 m² (hard minimum) and office at 9,000 m².
reasoning_trace.evidence: [min_bvo_productive_industry] requires ≥12,000 m² maakindustrie (a floor, not a ceiling); [max_bvo_office] caps office at 9,000 m². Both align with [target_bvo_maakindustrie] and [target_bvo_kantoren] in the toelichting.

reasoning_trace.decision: Apply tenure split 30% sociale huur / 40% middenhuur / 30% vrije sector consistent with transformation policy.
reasoning_trace.evidence: [programme_social_housing_transformation_30_percent], [programme_middeldure_transformation_40_percent], [programme_dure_transformation_30_percent] — transformation projects in Amsterdam follow 30/40/30 not the 40/40/20 new-build default.

reasoning_trace.decision: Bias unit sizes toward compact 1br/2br units in the 40–80 m² range.
reasoning_trace.evidence: [cbs_demographics: Population 4075, Households 2135, Avg household size 1.9, median age 39.4] indicates a buurt dominated by 1–2 person households; [min_middeldure_huur_go] sets a 40 m² floor for middenhuur units.

reasoning_trace.decision: Acknowledge 3D BAG unavailability and rely on 2D BAG + document height envelope (21–70 m, with one tower to 70 m).
reasoning_trace.evidence: Geo context notes 'pdok_3d_bag' failed (HTTP 400) and '3D BAG data available: False'. Height envelope derived from [building_height_range], [max_height_draka_terrein] (71 m TAR/radar cap), [max_height_exception_70] (one building to 70 m), and [hamerblok_base_height_south] (21 m base).

reasoning_trace.decision: Set total parking demand at 595 spaces (including 38 carshare).
reasoning_trace.evidence: [parking_car_total] explicitly states 595 parking spaces are required for the worst-case functieprogramma; [parking_car_shared] confirms 38 of those are deelauto's. Consistent with low norms [parking_norm_residential]=0.3, [parking_norm_sociale_huur]=0.1, [parking_norm_kantoor]=0.6/100m², and the autoluw character of Hamerkwartier supported by OSM transit access (bus 76 m, metro/train 377 m).

reasoning_trace.decision: Mixed-use programme aligns with surrounding built context.
reasoning_trace.evidence: [pdok_bag: 1000 buildings within 1000 m radius, year-built range 1888–2022] shows a transforming former industrial fabric; [osm: 35 playgrounds, 6 schools, 6 supermarkets, 10 parks nearby] confirms the neighbourhood already supports family/residential uses, justifying the 79% residential share within the 151,400 m² cap.

reasons: Programme totals and use caps are explicitly stated in the bestemmingsplan regels and toelichting
Tenure split anchored in explicit transformation policy
Parking demand stated verbatim in toelichting
Unit typology split partly designer judgment; 3D BAG context missing
